codeReview.summary: ProcessingScreen behavior is implemented, but password handling is functionally broken in the actual app state flow. The password modal is mounted inside DropScreen, while detection immediately transitions the app to LOADING (ProcessingScreen), so encrypted PDFs cannot show the modal and the load flow blocks waiting for password input.

issues.description: Encrypted-PDF flow cannot be completed: `DETECTION_START` sets app state to `LOADING` before `detectDocument` can request password, and App routes `LOADING` to `ProcessingScreen` (`src/components/App.tsx:23-24`). The `PasswordModal` is rendered only inside `DropScreen` (`src/components/DropScreen.tsx:182`), so when `onPassword` fires (`src/components/DropScreen.tsx:48-52`) there is no mounted modal to collect input. Result: expected behaviors for password prompt/retry/cancel are not reachable, and detection can hang waiting for password.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature-specific browser verification for encrypted PDF flow was not exercised end-to-end, which allowed a state-routing regression to pass despite tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session 776c1763-879c-469d-80af-34b36f5d3d37 shows state forcing and modal simulation (e.g., setting LOADING/PROCESSING via eval and notes about simulating DropScreen password modal signal) rather than dropping a password-protected PDF as described in `.factory/library/user-testing.md` key flow #6.

summary: Review result: fail. The processing UI exists, but encrypted PDF password handling is blocked by mounting the modal in DropScreen while LOADING renders ProcessingScreen; this prevents required password behaviors from working in real flow.
